context: =  sporadic very rare abnormality Prevalence:   5.5÷1,000,000 live births; M÷F = 2.7÷1 Cause:   failure of fusion of lateral thoracic folds + transverse diaphragmatic septum with variable extension inferiorly during days 14-18 of embryonic life Associated with: (a)  chromosomal anomalies: trisomies 13, 18, Turner syndrome (b)  facial defects: cleft lip or palate (c)  CNS anomalies: exencephaly, encephalocele, spina bifida, hydrocephalus (d)  musculoskeletal anomalies: clubfoot, absent radius √  exteriorization of heart Prognosis:   death within a few days after birth
